paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number
summary: Problem 16 asks two questions: whether all digraphs are $\kappa$-maderian (large strong connectivity forces subdivisions) and whether all digraphs are $\kappa'$-maderian (large strong arc-connectivity forces subdivisions). The $\kappa'$-maderian question was answered negatively by Gishboliner, Steiner, and Szabó (arXiv:2008.13224, Propositions 10--11), who showed that neither $\overleftrightarrow{K_4}$ nor $\overleftrightarrow{S_4}$ is $\kappa'$-maderian. The $\kappa$-maderian question remains open; Thomassen's Conjecture 17 from the source paper would imply a positive answer for that part.

since_posted.title: Oriented cycles in digraphs of large outdegree
since_posted.authors: Lior Gishboliner, Raphael Steiner, Tibor Szabó
since_posted.claim: Proves (Propositions 10 and 11) that neither $\overleftrightarrow{K_4}$ nor $\overleftrightarrow{S_4}$ is $\kappa'$-maderian, answering the $\kappa'$-maderian half of Problem 16 negatively; the $\kappa$-maderian question is noted as remaining open.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch confirmed the paper exists and addresses open questions from Aboulker et al. Abstract does not detail Propositions 10--11 on κ'-maderianness explicitly; specific claim taken from the fuzz=80 internal reference, which is treated as reliable. The two fuzz=79 entries for the same arXiv ID appear to correspond to different conjectures in the source paper (Conjecture 5 on oriented cycles; mader_{δ+} bound).

WebFetch confirmed the paper focuses on mader_χ (dichromatic number) results for octi/cactus digraphs. Its internal references claiming it 'fully resolves the conjecture' refer to a different conjecture (mader_χ for cycle orientations), not to Problem 16's κ/κ'-maderianness questions.
Not fetched within the 5-call cap. Internal reference indicates the paper only cites the conjecture as broader context without direct progress.

search_queries: kappa-maderian digraph strong connectivity subdivision Mader 1610.00876
notes: The three internal references for arXiv:2008.13224 are contradictory because the fuzzy matcher extracted text associated with different problems in the source paper. The fuzz=80 entry is the most specific to Problem 16. The κ-maderian question (strong connectivity version) remains open as of the latest literature found. No additional follow-up papers beyond arXiv:2008.13224 were found in the web search.
